sections.paragraph:
κατακλάω 1 aor. κατέκλασα; aor. pass. 3 sg. κατεκλάσθη Ezk 19:12 (s. κλάω; Hom. et al.; Philo, Somn. 2, 236; Jos., Bell. 7, 204, Ant. 2, 305) break in pieces τοὺς ἄρτους Mk 6:41; Lk 9:16.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 518
